1	from typing import Optional, Tuple
2	
3	import numpy as np
4	
5	from genai_bench.scenarios.base import (
6	    EmbeddingDistribution,
7	    ReRankDistribution,
8	    Scenario,
9	    TextDistribution,
10	    parse_params_str,
11	)
12	
13	
14	class NormalDistribution(Scenario):
15	    """
16	    Normal distribution
17	    e.g.
18	    N(300,150)/(480,240)
19	    """
20	
21	    scenario_type = TextDistribution.NORMAL
22	    validation_pattern = r"^N\(\d+,\d+\)\/\(\d+,\d+\)$"
23	
24	    def __init__(
25	        self,
26	        mean_input_tokens: int,
27	        stddev_input_tokens: int,
28	        mean_output_tokens: int,
29	        stddev_output_tokens: int,
30	    ):
31	        self.mean_input_tokens = mean_input_tokens
32	        self.stddev_input_tokens = stddev_input_tokens
33	        self.mean_output_tokens = mean_output_tokens
34	        self.stddev_output_tokens = stddev_output_tokens
35	
36	    def sample(self) -> Tuple[int, int]:
37	        num_input_tokens = max(
38	            1,
39	            int(np.random.normal(self.mean_input_tokens, self.stddev_input_tokens)),
40	        )
41	        num_output_tokens = max(
42	            2,
43	            int(np.random.normal(self.mean_output_tokens, self.stddev_output_tokens)),
44	        )
45	        return num_input_tokens, num_output_tokens
46	
47	    def to_string(self) -> str:
48	        return (
49	            f"N({self.mean_input_tokens},{self.stddev_input_tokens})/"
50	            f"({self.mean_output_tokens},{self.stddev_output_tokens})"
51	        )
52	
53	    @classmethod
54	    def parse(cls, params_str: str) -> "NormalDistribution":
55	        (mean_input, stddev_input), (mean_output, stddev_output) = parse_params_str(
56	            params_str
57	        )
58	        return cls(
59	            mean_input_tokens=mean_input,
60	            stddev_input_tokens=stddev_input,
61	            mean_output_tokens=mean_output,
62	            stddev_output_tokens=stddev_output,
63	        )
64	
65	
66	class UniformDistribution(Scenario):
67	    """
68	    Uniform distribution
69	    e.g.
70	    U(100,200)/(200,300),
71	    U(1000,100)
72	    """
73	
74	    scenario_type = TextDistribution.UNIFORM
75	    validation_pattern = r"^U\(\d+,\d+\)(?:\/\(\d+,\d+\))?$"
76	
77	    def __init__(
78	        self,
79	        max_input_tokens: int,
80	        max_output_tokens: int,
81	        min_input_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
82	        min_output_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
83	    ):
84	        self.min_input_tokens = min_input_tokens
85	        self.max_input_tokens = max_input_tokens
86	        self.min_output_tokens = min_output_tokens
87	        self.max_output_tokens = max_output_tokens
88	
89	    def sample(self) -> Tuple[int, int]:
90	        num_input_tokens = max(
91	            1,
92	            int(np.random.uniform(self.min_input_tokens or 1, self.max_input_tokens)),
93	        )
94	        num_output_tokens = max(
95	            2,
96	            int(np.random.uniform(self.min_output_tokens or 1, self.max_output_tokens)),
97	        )
98	        return num_input_tokens, num_output_tokens
99	
100	    def to_string(self) -> str:
101	        if self.min_input_tokens and self.min_output_tokens:
102	            return (
103	                f"U({self.min_input_tokens},{self.max_input_tokens})/"
104	                f"({self.min_output_tokens},{self.max_output_tokens})"
105	            )
106	        else:
107	            return f"U({self.max_input_tokens},{self.max_output_tokens})"
108	
109	    @classmethod
110	    def parse(cls, params_str: str) -> "UniformDistribution":
111	        parsed_parts = parse_params_str(params_str)
112	        if len(parsed_parts) == 2:
113	            (min_input, max_input), (min_output, max_output) = parsed_parts
114	            return cls(
115	                min_input_tokens=min_input,
116	                max_input_tokens=max_input,
117	                min_output_tokens=min_output,
118	                max_output_tokens=max_output,
119	            )
120	        else:
121	            (
122	                max_input,
123	                max_output,
124	            ) = parsed_parts[0]
125	            return cls(
126	                max_input_tokens=max_input,
127	                max_output_tokens=max_output,
128	            )
129	
130	
131	class DeterministicDistribution(Scenario):
132	    """
133	    Deterministic Distribution, aka constant
134	    e.g.
135	    D(100,1000)
136	    """
137	
138	    scenario_type = TextDistribution.DETERMINISTIC
139	    validation_pattern = r"^D\(\d+,\d+\)$"
140	
141	    def __init__(self, num_input_tokens: int, num_output_tokens: int):
142	        self.num_input_tokens = num_input_tokens
143	        self.num_output_tokens = num_output_tokens
144	
145	    def sample(self) -> Tuple[int, int]:
146	        return self.num_input_tokens, self.num_output_tokens
147	
148	    def to_string(self) -> str:
149	        return f"D({self.num_input_tokens},{self.num_output_tokens})"
150	
151	    @classmethod
152	    def parse(cls, params_str: str) -> "DeterministicDistribution":
153	        num_input, num_output = parse_params_str(params_str)[0]
154	        return cls(
155	            num_input_tokens=num_input,
156	            num_output_tokens=num_output,
157	        )
158	
159	
160	class EmbeddingScenario(Scenario):
161	    """
162	    A class to represent an embedding scenario
163	    e.g. E(tokens_per_document)
164	    """
165	
166	    scenario_type = EmbeddingDistribution.EMBEDDING
167	    validation_pattern = r"^E\(\d+\)$"
168	
169	    def __init__(self, tokens_per_document: int):
170	        self.tokens_per_document = tokens_per_document
171	
172	    def sample(self) -> int:
173	        """Returns tokens per document"""
174	        return self.tokens_per_document
175	
176	    def to_string(self) -> str:
177	        """
178	        Returns the embedding scenario object back in its string representation.
179	        For example E(1024).
180	        """
181	        return f"E({self.tokens_per_document})"
182	
183	    @classmethod
184	    def parse(cls, params_str: str) -> "EmbeddingScenario":
185	        """
186	        Parse the embedding scenario from a string, e.g. E(1024)
187	        """
188	        tokens_per_document = int(params_str[1:-1])
189	        return cls(tokens_per_document=tokens_per_document)
190	
191	
192	class ReRankScenario(Scenario):
193	    """
194	    A class to represent re-rank scenario
195	    e.g. R(tokens_per_document,tokens_per_query)
196	    """
197	
198	    scenario_type = ReRankDistribution.RE_RANK
199	    validation_pattern = r"^R\(\d+,\d+\)$"
200	
201	    def __init__(self, tokens_per_document: int, tokens_per_query: int):
202	        self.tokens_per_document = tokens_per_document
203	        self.tokens_per_query = tokens_per_query
204	
205	    def sample(self) -> Tuple[int, int]:
206	        """Returns tokens per document"""
207	        return self.tokens_per_document, self.tokens_per_query
208	
209	    def to_string(self) -> str:
210	        """
211	        Returns the re-rank scenario object back in its string representation.
212	        For example R(1024,100).
213	        """
214	        return f"R({self.tokens_per_document},{self.tokens_per_query})"
215	
216	    @classmethod
217	    def parse(cls, params_str: str) -> "ReRankScenario":
218	        """
219	        Parse the re-rank scenario from a string, e.g. R(1024,100)
220	        """
221	        tokens_per_document, tokens_per_query = parse_params_str(params_str)[0]
222	        return cls(
223	            tokens_per_document=tokens_per_document, tokens_per_query=tokens_per_query
224	        )

1	"""Dataset configuration models for flexible dataset loading."""
2	
3	import json
4	from pathlib import Path
5	from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
6	
7	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
8	
9	
10	class DatasetSourceConfig(BaseModel):
11	    """Configuration for a dataset source.
12	
13	    Supports multiple dataset source types:
14	    - file: Local files (txt, csv, json)
15	    - huggingface: HuggingFace Hub datasets
16	    - custom: Custom dataset loaders
17	    """
18	
19	    type: str = Field(
20	        ..., description="Dataset source type: 'file', 'huggingface', or 'custom'"
21	    )
22	    path: Optional[str] = Field(
23	        None, description="Path to dataset (file path or HuggingFace ID)"
24	    )
25	
26	    # For file sources
27	    file_format: Optional[str] = Field(
28	        None, description="File format: 'csv', 'txt', 'json'"
29	    )
30	
31	    # For HuggingFace sources - accepts ANY parameter that load_dataset supports
32	    huggingface_kwargs: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = Field(
33	        None,
34	        description="Keyword arguments passed directly to HuggingFace load_dataset",
35	    )
36	
37	    # For custom sources
38	    loader_class: Optional[str] = Field(
39	        None, description="Python import path for custom dataset loader"
40	    )
41	    loader_kwargs: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = Field(
42	        None, description="Keyword arguments for custom loader"
43	    )
44	
45	    @field_validator("type")
46	    def validate_type(cls, v):
47	        valid_types = {"file", "huggingface", "custom"}
48	        if v not in valid_types:
49	            raise ValueError(f"Dataset source type must be one of {valid_types}")
50	        return v
51	
52	
53	class DatasetConfig(BaseModel):
54	    """Complete dataset configuration."""
55	
56	    source: DatasetSourceConfig
57	    prompt_column: Optional[str] = Field(
58	        None, description="Column name containing prompts"
59	    )
60	    image_column: Optional[str] = Field(
61	        None, description="Column name containing images"
62	    )
63	    prompt_lambda: Optional[str] = Field(
64	        None,
65	        description="Lambda expression string, "
66	        'e.g. \'lambda item: f"Question: {item["question"]}"\'',
67	    )
68	    unsafe_allow_large_images: bool = Field(
69	        False,
70	        description="Overrides pillows internal DDOS protection",
71	    )
72	
73	    @classmethod
74	    def from_file(cls, config_path: str) -> "DatasetConfig":
75	        """Load configuration from a JSON file."""
76	        with open(config_path, "r") as f:
77	            data = json.load(f)
78	        return cls(**data)
79	
80	    @classmethod
81	    def from_cli_args(
82	        cls,
83	        dataset_path: Optional[str] = None,
84	        prompt_column: Optional[str] = None,
85	        image_column: Optional[str] = None,
86	        **kwargs,
87	    ) -> "DatasetConfig":
88	        """Create configuration from CLI arguments for backward compatibility."""
89	        if dataset_path is None:
90	            # Default to built-in sonnet.txt
91	            dataset_path = str(Path(__file__).parent / "sonnet.txt")
92	            source_type = "file"
93	            file_format = "txt"
94	        else:
95	            # Determine source type from path
96	            path = Path(dataset_path)
97	            if path.exists():
98	                source_type = "file"
99	                if path.suffix == ".csv":
100	                    file_format = "csv"
101	                elif path.suffix == ".txt":
102	                    file_format = "txt"
103	                elif path.suffix == ".json":
104	                    file_format = "json"
105	                else:
106	                    raise ValueError(f"Unsupported file format: {path.suffix}")
107	            else:
108	                # Assume it's a HuggingFace ID
109	                source_type = "huggingface"
110	                file_format = None
111	
112	        source_config = DatasetSourceConfig(
113	            type=source_type,
114	            path=dataset_path,
115	            file_format=file_format,
116	            huggingface_kwargs=None,
117	            loader_class=None,
118	            loader_kwargs=None,
119	        )
120	
121	        return cls(
122	            source=source_config,
123	            prompt_column=prompt_column,
124	            image_column=image_column,
125	            prompt_lambda=None,
126	            unsafe_allow_large_images=False,
127	        )
128

105	def validate_dataset_path_callback(ctx, param, value):
106	    """Validate the dataset path."""
107	    task = ctx.params.get("task")
108	    if not task:
109	        raise click.BadParameter(
110	            "The '--task' option is required but was not provided."
111	        )
112	
113	    input_modality, output_modality = task.split("-to-")
114	    if "image" in input_modality and value is None:
115	        # Check if dataset_config is provided as alternative
116	        dataset_config = ctx.params.get("dataset_config")
117	        if dataset_config is None:
118	            raise click.BadParameter(
119	                '--dataset-path is required when --task includes "image" input '
120	                "modality and --dataset-config is not provided."
121	            )
122	        else:
123	            logger.warning(
124	                "Using dataset configuration file for image task. "
125	                "Ensure your config file specifies the correct image dataset source."
126	            )
127	    return value

131	    """Validate dataset configuration file."""

133	        return None
134	
135	    try:

137	        return value
138	    except Exception as e:

151	def validate_traffic_scenario_callback(ctx, param, value):
152	    """Validate and assign traffic scenarios based on the task."""
153	    task = ctx.params.get("task")
154	    if not task:
155	        raise click.BadParameter(
156	            "The '--task' option is required but was not provided."
157	        )
158	    if value:
159	        return [validate_scenario_callback(v) for v in value]
160	    # If user provided a dataset and no scenario, prefer dataset mode (no data shaping)
161	    if ctx.params.get("dataset_path") or ctx.params.get("dataset_config"):
162	        return ["dataset"]
163	    if task not in DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_BY_TASK:
164	        raise click.BadParameter(
165	            f"No default traffic scenarios defined for task '{task}'"
166	        )
167	
168	    logger.info(f"Using default traffic scenarios for task {task}")
169	    return DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_BY_TASK[task]

172	def validate_tokenizer(model_tokenizer):
173	    """Validate and load the tokenizer, either locally or from HuggingFace.
174	
175	    The function now tries an **anonymous** download first to support public
176	    repositories without requiring an ``HF_TOKEN``. Authentication is only
177	    enforced when the Hugging Face Hub returns *401* or *403* errors.
178	    """
179	    if isinstance(model_tokenizer, str) and Path(model_tokenizer).exists():
180	        return AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_tokenizer)
181	    hf_token = os.environ.get("HF_TOKEN")
182	
183	    try:
184	        return AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_tokenizer, token=hf_token)
185	    except (HfHubHTTPError, OSError) as e:
186	        is_auth_error = False
187	
188	        if isinstance(e, OSError):
189	            if (
190	                "gated repo" in str(e)
191	                or "private repository" in str(e)
192	                and "make sure to pass a token" in str(e)
193	            ):
194	                is_auth_error = True
195	        elif isinstance(e, HfHubHTTPError) and e.response is not None:
196	            is_auth_error = e.response.status_code in {401, 403, 404}
197	
198	        if is_auth_error and hf_token is None:
199	            raise click.BadParameter(
200	                "Hugging Face requires authentication for this tokenizer. "
201	                "Please export HF_TOKEN with a valid access token and retry."
202	            ) from e
203	
204	        # Propagate all other errors unchanged
205	        raise
206

208	def validate_iteration_params(ctx, param, value) -> str:
209	    """
210	    Validate and determine iteration parameters based on task type.
211	
212	    Args:
213	        ctx: Click context
214	        param: Click parameter
215	        value: Current parameter value (iteration_type)
216	
217	    Returns:
218	        str: The validated iteration_type
219	    """
220	    task = ctx.params.get("task")
221	    num_concurrency = ctx.params.get("num_concurrency", [])
222	    batch_size = ctx.params.get("batch_size", [])
223	
224	    # For text-to-embeddings tasks, always use batch_size iteration
225	    if task == "text-to-embeddings" or task == "text-to-rerank":
226	        if value != "batch_size":
227	            click.echo("Note: Using batch_size iteration for text-to-embeddings task")
228	        batch_size = batch_size or DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZES
229	        value = "batch_size"
230	        num_concurrency = [1]
231	
232	    # For all other tasks, use num_concurrency iteration
233	    else:
234	        if value != "num_concurrency":
235	            click.echo(f"Note: Using num_concurrency iteration for {task} task")
236	        num_concurrency = num_concurrency or DEFAULT_NUM_CONCURRENCIES
237	        value = "num_concurrency"
238	        batch_size = [1]
239	
240	    # Update context with validated values
241	    ctx.params.update(
242	        {
243	            "iteration_type": value,
244	            "batch_size": batch_size,
245	            "num_concurrency": num_concurrency,
246	        }
247	    )
248	
249	    return value

257	def validate_api_backend(ctx, param, value):
258	    """
259	    Validate the selected API backend and set the corresponding user class.
260	    """
261	    api_backend = value.lower()
262	    user_class = API_BACKEND_USER_MAP.get(api_backend)
263	    if not user_class:
264	        raise click.BadParameter(f"{value} is not a supported API backend.")
265	
266	    if ctx.obj is None:
267	        ctx.obj = {}
268	    ctx.obj["user_class"] = user_class
269	
270	    return api_backend

273	def validate_api_key(ctx, param, value):
274	    """Validate API key based on backend."""
275	    api_backend = ctx.params.get("api_backend")
276	
277	    if not api_backend:
278	        raise click.BadParameter("api_backend must be specified before api_key")
279	
280	    # Backends that require API key
281	    api_key_required = [OpenAIUser.BACKEND_NAME, "vllm", "sglang"]
282	
283	    # Backends that don't use traditional API key
284	    no_api_key = [
285	        OCIOpenAIUser.BACKEND_NAME,
286	        OCICohereUser.BACKEND_NAME,
287	        OCICohereV2User.BACKEND_NAME,
288	        OCIGenAIUser.BACKEND_NAME,
289	        CohereUser.BACKEND_NAME,
290	        AWSBedrockUser.BACKEND_NAME,
291	        GCPVertexUser.BACKEND_NAME,
292	    ]
293	
294	    # Azure OpenAI can use API key or Azure AD
295	    if api_backend in api_key_required:
296	        if not value:
297	            raise click.BadParameter(f"API key is required for {api_backend} backend")
298	    elif api_backend == AzureOpenAIUser.BACKEND_NAME:
299	        # Azure can use API key or Azure AD - validated in model auth options
300	        pass
301	    elif api_backend in no_api_key:
302	        if value:
303	            click.echo(
304	                f"Warning: API key is not used for {api_backend} backend "
305	                f"as it uses cloud-specific authentication",
306	                err=True,
307	            )
308	        return None
309	
310	    return value
311	
312	
313	def validate_task(ctx, param, value):
314	    """
315	    Validate the selected task and ensure compatibility with the API backend.
316	    """
317	    task = value.lower()
318	
319	    # Ensure API backend has been validated
320	    user_class = ctx.obj.get("user_class") if ctx.obj else None
321	    if not user_class:
322	        raise click.BadParameter(
323	            "API backend is not set. Please provide a valid --api-backend "
324	            "before --task."
325	        )
326	
327	    # Check task compatibility at the user class level
328	    if not user_class.is_task_supported(task):
329	        supported_tasks = ", ".join(user_class.supported_tasks.keys())
330	        raise click.BadParameter(
331	            f"Task '{task}' is not supported by the selected API backend. "
332	            f"Supported tasks are: {supported_tasks}."
333	        )
334	
335	    # Some tasks are only supported by specific backends within the same
336	    # user class. For example, text-to-rerank requires a /rerank endpoint
337	    # which is available on vLLM/SGLang but not on the standard OpenAI API.
338	    backend_task_restrictions = {
339	        "text-to-rerank": {"vllm", "sglang"},
340	    }
341	    api_backend = ctx.params.get("api_backend", "").lower()
342	    allowed_backends = backend_task_restrictions.get(task)
343	    if allowed_backends and api_backend not in allowed_backends:
344	        raise click.BadParameter(
345	            f"Task '{task}' is not supported by the '{api_backend}' backend. "
346	            f"Supported backends for this task: {', '.join(sorted(allowed_backends))}."
347	        )
348	
349	    # Store the user task function in the context
350	    ctx.obj["user_task"] = getattr(user_class, user_class.supported_tasks[task])
351	
352	    return task
353	
354	
355	def set_model_from_tokenizer(ctx, param, value):
356	    """
357	    Infer the model name from the tokenizer if not explicitly provided.
358	    """
359	    model_tokenizer = ctx.params.get("model_tokenizer")
360	    return value or model_tokenizer.split("/")[-1]

363	def validate_additional_request_params(ctx, param, value):
364	    """Validate and parse additional request parameters in JSON format."""
365	    if value is None:
366	        return {}  # Return an empty dictionary to be parsed in Sampler
367	    try:
368	        value = json.loads(value)
369	        if "ignore_eos" in value and not value["ignore_eos"]:
370	            warning_msg = (
371	                "You have set ignore_eos to False. This will cause inaccurate "
372	                "num_output_tokens because the generation can stop before "
373	                "reaching the num_output_tokens you passed. Unless you are "
374	                "doing correctness test, it is recommended to set ignore_eos "
375	                "to True."
376	            )
377	            logger.warning(warning_msg)
378	            click.secho(warning_msg, fg="yellow", bold=True)
379	            if click.confirm(
380	                "Do you want to reset the ignore_eos to True?", default=False
381	            ):
382	                value["ignore_eos"] = True
383	
384	        if ctx.params.get("task") == "text-to-image" and "n" in value:
385	            raise click.BadParameter(
386	                "Set image count via --traffic-scenario I(width,height,num_images)."
387	            )
388	
389	        return value
390	    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
391	        raise click.BadParameter(
392	            f"Invalid JSON string in `--additional-request-params`: {str(e)}"
393	        ) from e
394	
395	
396	def validate_filter_criteria(ctx, param, value):
397	    """Parse and validate the filter criteria provided by the user."""
398	    if value is None:
399	        return {}
400	    try:
401	        return json.loads(value)
402	    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
403	        raise click.BadParameter(
404	            f"Invalid JSON string in `--filter-criteria`: {str(e)}"
405	        ) from e
406	
407	
408	def validate_object_storage_options(ctx, param, value):
409	    """Validate object storage options."""
410	    if (
411	        param.name == "upload_results"
412	        and value
413	        and not ctx.params.get("storage_bucket")
414	    ):
415	        raise click.UsageError(
416	            "You must provide a storage bucket name (--storage-bucket) "
417	            "when uploading results."
418	        )
419	    return value
420	
421	
422	def validate_warmup_cooldown_ratio_options(ctx, param, value):
423	    """Validate warmup and cooldown options."""
424	    warmup_ratio = ctx.params.get("warmup_ratio") or 0.0
425	    cooldown_ratio = value or 0.0

import json
import os
from pathlib import Path

import click
from huggingface_hub.utils import HfHubHTTPError
from transformers import AutoTokenizer

from genai_bench.data.config import DatasetConfig
from genai_bench.logging import init_logger
from genai_bench.scenarios.base import Scenario
from genai_bench.user.aws_bedrock_user import AWSBedrockUser
from genai_bench.user.azure_openai_user import AzureOpenAIUser
from genai_bench.user.cohere_user import CohereUser
from genai_bench.user.gcp_vertex_user import GCPVertexUser
from genai_bench.user.oci_cohere_user import OCICohereUser
from genai_bench.user.oci_cohere_v2_user import OCICohereV2User
from genai_bench.user.oci_genai_user import OCIGenAIUser
from genai_bench.user.oci_openai_user import OCIOpenAIUser
from genai_bench.user.openai_user import OpenAIUser
from genai_bench.user.together_user import TogetherUser

logger = init_logger(__name__)

API_BACKEND_USER_MAP = {
    OpenAIUser.BACKEND_NAME: OpenAIUser,
    OCIOpenAIUser.BACKEND_NAME: OCIOpenAIUser,
    OCICohereUser.BACKEND_NAME: OCICohereUser,
    OCICohereV2User.BACKEND_NAME: OCICohereV2User,
    OCIGenAIUser.BACKEND_NAME: OCIGenAIUser,
    CohereUser.BACKEND_NAME: CohereUser,
    AWSBedrockUser.BACKEND_NAME: AWSBedrockUser,
    AzureOpenAIUser.BACKEND_NAME: AzureOpenAIUser,
    GCPVertexUser.BACKEND_NAME: GCPVertexUser,
    TogetherUser.BACKEND_NAME: TogetherUser,
    "vllm": OpenAIUser,  # vLLM uses OpenAI-compatible API
    "sglang": OpenAIUser,  # SGLang uses OpenAI-compatible API
}

DEFAULT_NUM_CONCURRENCIES = [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256]
DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZES = [1, 2, 4, 8, 24, 48, 96]

DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_FOR_CHAT = [
    "N(480,240)/(300,150)",
    "D(100,100)",
    "D(100,1000)",
    "D(2000,200)",
    "D(7800,200)",
]


DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_FOR_VISION = [
    "I(512,512)",
    "I(1024,512)",
    "I(2048,2048)",
]

DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_FOR_EMBEDDING = [
    "E(64)",
    "E(128)",
    "E(256)",
    "E(512)",
    "E(1024)",
]

DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_FOR_RERANK = [
    "R(64,100)",
    "R(128,100)",
    "R(256,100)",
    "R(512,100)",
    "R(1024,100)",
    "R(2096,100)",
    "R(4096,100)",
]

DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_FOR_IMAGE_GENERATION = [
    "I(512,512)",
    "I(1024,1024)",
    "I(1024,1792)",
]

DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_FOR_SPEECH = [
    "A(100)",
    "A(500)",
    "A(1000)",
    "A(2000)",
    "A(4000)",
]

DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_BY_TASK = {
    "text-to-text": DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_FOR_CHAT,
    "text-to-rerank": DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_FOR_RERANK,
    "text-to-image": DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_FOR_IMAGE_GENERATION,
    "text-to-speech": DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_FOR_SPEECH,
    "image-text-to-text": DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_FOR_VISION,
    "text-to-embeddings": DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_FOR_EMBEDDING,
    "image-to-embeddings": DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_FOR_VISION,
    # add other tasks and default scenarios as needed
}


# -------------------------
# General Validation Functions
# -------------------------
def validate_dataset_path_callback(ctx, param, value):
    """Validate the dataset path."""
    task = ctx.params.get("task")
    if not task:
        raise click.BadParameter(
            "The '--task' option is required but was not provided."
        )

    input_modality, output_modality = task.split("-to-")
    if "image" in input_modality and value is None:
        # Check if dataset_config is provided as alternative
        dataset_config = ctx.params.get("dataset_config")
        if dataset_config is None:
            raise click.BadParameter(
                '--dataset-path is required when --task includes "image" input '
                "modality and --dataset-config is not provided."
            )
        else:
            logger.warning(
                "Using dataset configuration file for image task. "
                "Ensure your config file specifies the correct image dataset source."
            )
    return value


def validate_dataset_config(ctx, param, value):
    """Validate dataset configuration file."""
    if value is None:
        return None

    try:
        _ = DatasetConfig.from_file(value)
        return value
    except Exception as e:
        raise click.BadParameter(f"Invalid dataset configuration file: {str(e)}") from e


def validate_scenario_callback(scenario):
    """Validate an individual traffic scenario."""
    try:
        Scenario.validate(scenario)
        return scenario
    except ValueError as e:
        raise click.BadParameter(str(e)) from e


def validate_traffic_scenario_callback(ctx, param, value):
    """Validate and assign traffic scenarios based on the task."""
    task = ctx.params.get("task")
    if not task:
        raise click.BadParameter(
            "The '--task' option is required but was not provided."
        )
    if value:
        return [validate_scenario_callback(v) for v in value]
    # If user provided a dataset and no scenario, prefer dataset mode (no data shaping)
    if ctx.params.get("dataset_path") or ctx.params.get("dataset_config"):
        return ["dataset"]
    if task not in DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_BY_TASK:
        raise click.BadParameter(
            f"No default traffic scenarios defined for task '{task}'"
        )

    logger.info(f"Using default traffic scenarios for task {task}")
    return DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_BY_TASK[task]


def validate_tokenizer(model_tokenizer):
    """Validate and load the tokenizer, either locally or from HuggingFace.

    The function now tries an **anonymous** download first to support public
    repositories without requiring an ``HF_TOKEN``. Authentication is only
    enforced when the Hugging Face Hub returns *401* or *403* errors.
    """
    if isinstance(model_tokenizer, str) and Path(model_tokenizer).exists():
        return AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_tokenizer)
    hf_token = os.environ.get("HF_TOKEN")

    try:
        return AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_tokenizer, token=hf_token)
    except (HfHubHTTPError, OSError) as e:
        is_auth_error = False

        if isinstance(e, OSError):
            if (
                "gated repo" in str(e)
                or "private repository" in str(e)
                and "make sure to pass a token" in str(e)
            ):
                is_auth_error = True
        elif isinstance(e, HfHubHTTPError) and e.response is not None:
            is_auth_error = e.response.status_code in {401, 403, 404}

        if is_auth_error and hf_token is None:
            raise click.BadParameter(
                "Hugging Face requires authentication for this tokenizer. "
                "Please export HF_TOKEN with a valid access token and retry."
            ) from e

        # Propagate all other errors unchanged
        raise


def validate_iteration_params(ctx, param, value) -> str:
    """
    Validate and determine iteration parameters based on task type.

    Args:
        ctx: Click context
        param: Click parameter
        value: Current parameter value (iteration_type)

    Returns:
        str: The validated iteration_type
    """
    task = ctx.params.get("task")
    num_concurrency = ctx.params.get("num_concurrency", [])
    batch_size = ctx.params.get("batch_size", [])

    # For text-to-embeddings tasks, always use batch_size iteration
    if task == "text-to-embeddings" or task == "text-to-rerank":
        if value != "batch_size":
            click.echo("Note: Using batch_size iteration for text-to-embeddings task")
        batch_size = batch_size or DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZES
        value = "batch_size"
        num_concurrency = [1]

    # For all other tasks, use num_concurrency iteration
    else:
        if value != "num_concurrency":
            click.echo(f"Note: Using num_concurrency iteration for {task} task")
        num_concurrency = num_concurrency or DEFAULT_NUM_CONCURRENCIES
        value = "num_concurrency"
        batch_size = [1]

    # Update context with validated values
    ctx.params.update(
        {
            "iteration_type": value,
            "batch_size": batch_size,
            "num_concurrency": num_concurrency,
        }
    )

    return value


# -------------------------
# CLI Validation Functions
# -------------------------


def validate_api_backend(ctx, param, value):
    """
    Validate the selected API backend and set the corresponding user class.
    """
    api_backend = value.lower()
    user_class = API_BACKEND_USER_MAP.get(api_backend)
    if not user_class:
        raise click.BadParameter(f"{value} is not a supported API backend.")

    if ctx.obj is None:
        ctx.obj = {}
    ctx.obj["user_class"] = user_class

    return api_backend


def validate_api_key(ctx, param, value):
    """Validate API key based on backend."""
    api_backend = ctx.params.get("api_backend")

    if not api_backend:
        raise click.BadParameter("api_backend must be specified before api_key")

    # Backends that require API key
    api_key_required = [OpenAIUser.BACKEND_NAME, "vllm", "sglang"]

    # Backends that don't use traditional API key
    no_api_key = [
        OCIOpenAIUser.BACKEND_NAME,
        OCICohereUser.BACKEND_NAME,
        OCICohereV2User.BACKEND_NAME,
        OCIGenAIUser.BACKEND_NAME,
        CohereUser.BACKEND_NAME,
        AWSBedrockUser.BACKEND_NAME,
        GCPVertexUser.BACKEND_NAME,
    ]

    # Azure OpenAI can use API key or Azure AD
    if api_backend in api_key_required:
        if not value:
            raise click.BadParameter(f"API key is required for {api_backend} backend")
    elif api_backend == AzureOpenAIUser.BACKEND_NAME:
        # Azure can use API key or Azure AD - validated in model auth options
        pass
    elif api_backend in no_api_key:
        if value:
            click.echo(
                f"Warning: API key is not used for {api_backend} backend "
                f"as it uses cloud-specific authentication",
                err=True,
            )
        return None

    return value


def validate_task(ctx, param, value):
    """
    Validate the selected task and ensure compatibility with the API backend.
    """
    task = value.lower()

    # Ensure API backend has been validated
    user_class = ctx.obj.get("user_class") if ctx.obj else None
    if not user_class:
        raise click.BadParameter(
            "API backend is not set. Please provide a valid --api-backend "
            "before --task."
        )

    # Check task compatibility at the user class level
    if not user_class.is_task_supported(task):
        supported_tasks = ", ".join(user_class.supported_tasks.keys())
        raise click.BadParameter(
            f"Task '{task}' is not supported by the selected API backend. "
            f"Supported tasks are: {supported_tasks}."
        )

    # Some tasks are only supported by specific backends within the same
    # user class. For example, text-to-rerank requires a /rerank endpoint
    # which is available on vLLM/SGLang but not on the standard OpenAI API.
    backend_task_restrictions = {
        "text-to-rerank": {"vllm", "sglang"},
    }
    api_backend = ctx.params.get("api_backend", "").lower()
    allowed_backends = backend_task_restrictions.get(task)
    if allowed_backends and api_backend not in allowed_backends:
        raise click.BadParameter(
            f"Task '{task}' is not supported by the '{api_backend}' backend. "
            f"Supported backends for this task: {', '.join(sorted(allowed_backends))}."
        )

    # Store the user task function in the context
    ctx.obj["user_task"] = getattr(user_class, user_class.supported_tasks[task])

    return task


def set_model_from_tokenizer(ctx, param, value):
    """
    Infer the model name from the tokenizer if not explicitly provided.
    """
    model_tokenizer = ctx.params.get("model_tokenizer")
    return value or model_tokenizer.split("/")[-1]


def validate_additional_request_params(ctx, param, value):
    """Validate and parse additional request parameters in JSON format."""
    if value is None:
        return {}  # Return an empty dictionary to be parsed in Sampler
    try:
        value = json.loads(value)
        if "ignore_eos" in value and not value["ignore_eos"]:
            warning_msg = (
                "You have set ignore_eos to False. This will cause inaccurate "
                "num_output_tokens because the generation can stop before "
                "reaching the num_output_tokens you passed. Unless you are "
                "doing correctness test, it is recommended to set ignore_eos "
                "to True."
            )
            logger.warning(warning_msg)
            click.secho(warning_msg, fg="yellow", bold=True)
            if click.confirm(
                "Do you want to reset the ignore_eos to True?", default=False
            ):
                value["ignore_eos"] = True

        if ctx.params.get("task") == "text-to-image" and "n" in value:
            raise click.BadParameter(
                "Set image count via --traffic-scenario I(width,height,num_images)."
            )

        return value
    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
        raise click.BadParameter(
            f"Invalid JSON string in `--additional-request-params`: {str(e)}"
        ) from e


def validate_filter_criteria(ctx, param, value):
    """Parse and validate the filter criteria provided by the user."""
    if value is None:
        return {}
    try:
        return json.loads(value)
    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
        raise click.BadParameter(
            f"Invalid JSON string in `--filter-criteria`: {str(e)}"
        ) from e


def validate_object_storage_options(ctx, param, value):
    """Validate object storage options."""
    if (
        param.name == "upload_results"
        and value
        and not ctx.params.get("storage_bucket")
    ):
        raise click.UsageError(
            "You must provide a storage bucket name (--storage-bucket) "
            "when uploading results."
        )
    return value


def validate_warmup_cooldown_ratio_options(ctx, param, value):
    """Validate warmup and cooldown options."""
    warmup_ratio = ctx.params.get("warmup_ratio") or 0.0
    cooldown_ratio = value or 0.0
    if warmup_ratio + cooldown_ratio >= 1.0:
        raise click.BadParameter(
            f"warmup_ratio({warmup_ratio}) + cooldown_ratio({cooldown_ratio}) "
            f"must be < 1.0.",
            param_hint=["--warmup-ratio", "--cooldown-ratio"],
        )
    return value


def _get_min_input_tokens(scenario: Scenario) -> int | None:
    """
    Get the minimum possible input tokens for a scenario.

    Returns:
        Minimum input tokens, or None if cannot be determined.
    """
    # Deterministic: exact value
    if hasattr(scenario, "num_input_tokens"):
        return scenario.num_input_tokens

    # Normal distribution: mean - 3*stddev (practical lower bound, ~99.7% of samples)
    if hasattr(scenario, "mean_input_tokens") and hasattr(
        scenario, "stddev_input_tokens"
    ):
        return max(1, scenario.mean_input_tokens - 3 * scenario.stddev_input_tokens)

    # Uniform distribution: min_input_tokens or 1
    if hasattr(scenario, "min_input_tokens"):
        return scenario.min_input_tokens or 1

    return None


def validate_prefix_options(
    prefix_len, prefix_ratio, task, dataset_path, dataset_config, traffic_scenario
):
    """
    Validate --prefix-len and --prefix-ratio options after CLI parsing completes.

    All validation (basic and contextual) happens here in one place.
    """
    # Check mutual exclusivity
    if prefix_len is not None and prefix_ratio is not None:
        raise click.UsageError(
            "--prefix-len and --prefix-ratio are mutually exclusive. Use only one."
        )

    # If neither is set, nothing to validate
    if prefix_len is None and prefix_ratio is None:
        return

    # Determine which option is being used
    option_name = "--prefix-len" if prefix_len is not None else "--prefix-ratio"

    # Task compatibility
    if task != "text-to-text":
        raise click.UsageError(
            f"{option_name} is only supported for text-to-text tasks, not '{task}'."
        )

    # Dataset mode compatibility
    if (dataset_path or dataset_config) and not traffic_scenario:
        raise click.UsageError(
            f"{option_name} requires a traffic scenario. "
            "Not supported with dataset mode."
        )

    # Validate traffic scenarios
    if traffic_scenario:
        # Reject if any scenario is "dataset"
        if "dataset" in traffic_scenario:
            raise click.UsageError(
                f"{option_name} is not supported with dataset mode. "
                f"Remove 'dataset' from --traffic-scenario or remove {option_name}."
            )

        # --prefix-ratio requires deterministic scenarios (ratio adapts per-request)
        # --prefix-len works with any scenario type (fixed prefix, variable suffix)
        if prefix_ratio is not None:
            non_deterministic = [
                s for s in traffic_scenario if not s.strip().startswith("D(")
            ]
            if non_deterministic:
                raise click.UsageError(
                    "--prefix-ratio requires all traffic scenarios "
                    "to be deterministic. Non-deterministic "
                    f"scenarios found: {', '.join(non_deterministic)}"
                )

        # For --prefix-len, validate against minimum possible input tokens
        # Supported: D(n,m), N(mean,std)/(m,s), U(min,max)/(m,n)
        # Unsupported: I(), E(), R()
        if prefix_len is not None:
            unsupported_scenarios = []
            invalid_scenarios = []
            for scenario_str in traffic_scenario:
                scenario = Scenario.from_string(scenario_str)
                min_input_tokens = _get_min_input_tokens(scenario)
                if min_input_tokens is None:
                    unsupported_scenarios.append(scenario_str)
                elif min_input_tokens < prefix_len:
                    invalid_scenarios.append(
                        f"{scenario_str} (min_input_tokens={min_input_tokens})"
                    )

            if unsupported_scenarios:
                raise click.UsageError(
                    "--prefix-len only supports text scenarios (D, N, U). "
                    f"Unsupported scenarios: {', '.join(unsupported_scenarios)}"
                )

            if invalid_scenarios:
                raise click.UsageError(
                    f"--prefix-len ({prefix_len}) must be <= minimum input tokens "
                    f"for all scenarios "
                    f"(D: num_input_tokens, N: mean-3*stddev, U: min_input_tokens). "
                    f"Invalid scenarios: {', '.join(invalid_scenarios)}"
                )

7	from openpyxl.utils import column_index_from_string, get_column_letter
8	from openpyxl.worksheet.worksheet import Worksheet

16	logger = init_logger(__name__)

28	def _group_empty_columns(wb: Workbook) -> None:
29	    """Group and collapse columns where all data cells are 0, None, or all-zero JSON."""
30	    target_sheets = {
31	        "Aggregated Metrics for Each Run",
32	        "Individual Request Metrics",
33	    }
34	    for sheet in wb:
35	        if sheet.title not in target_sheets or sheet.max_row <= 1:
36	            continue
37	        empty_cols = []
38	        for col_idx in range(1, sheet.max_column + 1):
39	            all_empty = True
40	            for row_idx in range(2, sheet.max_row + 1):
41	                value = sheet.cell(row=row_idx, column=col_idx).value
42	                if value is None or value == 0 or value == 0.0:
43	                    continue
44	                if isinstance(value, str) and value.startswith("{"):
45	                    parsed = json.loads(value)
46	                    if parsed and all(v is None or v == 0 for v in parsed.values()):
47	                        continue
48	                all_empty = False
49	                break
50	            if all_empty:
51	                empty_cols.append(col_idx)
52	
53	        # Group contiguous ranges and hide each column
54	        if not empty_cols:
55	            continue
56	        ranges = []
57	        start = empty_cols[0]
58	        prev = empty_cols[0]
59	        for col_idx in empty_cols[1:]:
60	            if col_idx == prev + 1:
61	                prev = col_idx
62	            else:
63	                ranges.append((start, prev))
64	                start = col_idx
65	                prev = col_idx
66	        ranges.append((start, prev))
67	
68	        for range_start, range_end in ranges:
69	            for col_idx in range(range_start, range_end + 1):
70	                col_letter = get_column_letter(col_idx)
71	                sheet.column_dimensions[col_letter].outlineLevel = 1
72	                sheet.column_dimensions[col_letter].hidden = True
73	
74	
75	def _rename_headers(wb: Workbook, rename_map: dict) -> None:
76	    """Rename header cells (row 1) across all sheets via substring replace."""
77	    for sheet in wb:
78	        for cell in sheet[1]:
79	            if cell.value:
80	                for old, new in rename_map.items():
81	                    if old in str(cell.value):
82	                        cell.value = str(cell.value).replace(old, new)
83

"""Storage authentication provider interface."""

from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from typing import Any, Dict, Optional


class StorageAuthProvider(ABC):
    """Base class for storage authentication providers."""

    @abstractmethod
    def get_client_config(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get storage service-specific client configuration.

        Returns:
            Dict[str, Any]: Configuration for storage client initialization
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def get_credentials(self) -> Any:
        """Get authentication credentials for storage operations.

        Returns:
            Any: Authentication credentials (e.g., access keys, signers)
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def get_storage_type(self) -> str:
        """Get the storage provider type.

        Returns:
            str: Storage type (e.g., 'oci', 'aws', 'azure', 'gcp', 'github')
        """
        pass

    def get_region(self) -> Optional[str]:
        """Get the region for storage operations.

        Returns:
            Optional[str]: Region or None if not applicable
        """
        return None

8	    """Utility class for converting time metrics between different units."""

10	    # Fields that contain time/latency values (in seconds internally)
11	    LATENCY_FIELDS = {"ttft", "tpot", "e2e_latency", "output_latency"}

25	        "p99",
26	    }
27	
28	    @staticmethod
29	    def convert_value(
30	        value: Optional[float], from_unit: str, to_unit: str
31	    ) -> Optional[float]:
32	        """
33	        Convert a time value between units.
34	
35	        Args:
36	            value: The time value to convert (can be None)
37	            from_unit: Source unit ('s' or 'ms')
38	            to_unit: Target unit ('s' or 'ms')
39	
40	        Returns:
41	            Converted value or None if input was None
42	        """
43	        if value is None:
44	            return None
45	
46	        if from_unit == to_unit:
47	            return value
48	
49	        if from_unit == "s" and to_unit == "ms":
50	            return value * 1000
51	        elif from_unit == "ms" and to_unit == "s":
52	            return value / 1000
53	        else:
54	            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported unit conversion: {from_unit} -> {to_unit}")

"""Flexible plotting system supporting user-defined plot configurations."""

import os
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from matplotlib.axes import Axes
from matplotlib.figure import Figure

from genai_bench.analysis.experiment_loader import ExperimentMetrics, MetricsData
from genai_bench.analysis.plot_config import PlotConfig, PlotConfigManager, PlotSpec
from genai_bench.analysis.plot_report import (
    get_scenario_data,
    plot_error_rates,
    plot_graph,
)
from genai_bench.logging import init_logger
from genai_bench.protocol import ExperimentMetadata
from genai_bench.time_units import TimeUnitConverter
from genai_bench.utils import sanitize_string

logger = init_logger(__name__)


class FlexiblePlotGenerator:
    """Generates plots using flexible configuration."""

    def __init__(self, config: PlotConfig):
        """Initialize with plot configuration and time unit."""
        self.config = config

    def generate_plots(
        self,
        run_data_list: List[Tuple[ExperimentMetadata, ExperimentMetrics]],
        group_key: str,
        experiment_folder: str,
        metrics_time_unit: str = "s",
    ) -> None:
        """Generate plots based on configuration."""
        # Extract source time unit from experiment metadata
        source_time_unit = (
            run_data_list[0][0].metrics_time_unit if run_data_list else "s"
        )

        if group_key == "none":
            self._plot_single_analysis(
                run_data_list, experiment_folder, metrics_time_unit, source_time_unit
            )
        elif group_key == "traffic_scenario":
            self._plot_by_scenario(
                run_data_list, experiment_folder, metrics_time_unit, source_time_unit
            )
        else:
            self._plot_by_group(
                run_data_list,
                group_key,
                experiment_folder,
                metrics_time_unit,
                source_time_unit,

file.content:
import logging
import multiprocessing
import os
import sys
from io import StringIO
from typing import List, Set, Tuple

from rich.console import Console
from rich.layout import Layout
from rich.live import Live
from rich.logging import RichHandler
from rich.panel import Panel


class RollingRichPanelHandler(RichHandler):
    """
    A log handler that emits the record using rich formatting and
    updates the most recent logs to the logs panel on the live dashboard.
    """

    def __init__(
        self, layout: Layout, panel_name="logs", max_entries=10, *args, **kwargs
    ):
        self.log_buffer = StringIO()
        kwargs["console"] = Console(file=self.log_buffer, width=150)
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        self.layout = layout
        self.panel_name = panel_name
        self.max_entries = max_entries

    def emit(self, record):
        # Continue normal RichHandler behavior
        super().emit(record)
        log_contents = self.log_buffer.getvalue()

        # Trim the log lines to only the last max_entries
        log_lines = log_contents.splitlines()
        last_10_lines = log_lines[-self.max_entries :]
        trimmed_log_contents = "\n".join(last_10_lines)
        self.layout[self.panel_name].update(
            Panel(trimmed_log_contents, title="Logs", border_style="red")
        )


class DelayedRichHandler(RichHandler):
    """
    A delayed RichHandler that buffers all the logs then flush them to console
    all at once later.

    During rich.live.Live context, all the logs that are captured by RichHandler

I now have a comprehensive and accurate understanding of the genai-bench project. The tutorial directory is empty (only .transcripts), so this is full mode and all lectures will be "new".

Let me now design the learning outline. The project is a medium-to-large Python benchmarking tool. Based on the planner rules, medium projects get 15-30 lectures. I'll design around 18-22 lectures organized into units.

Let me structure the architecture understanding:

**Project: genai-bench** — A token-level LLM serving benchmark tool built on Locust. CLI + Live UI Dashboard + Experiment Analyzer (Excel + Plots).

**Tech stack:**
- Python 3.10-3.12
- Locust (load testing framework) + gevent (concurrency)
- Click (CLI)
- Pydantic (data models / protocol)
- Rich (live UI)
- transformers (tokenizer), HuggingFace datasets
- numpy, pandas, openpyxl, matplotlib (analysis)
- Multi-cloud: OCI, AWS (boto3), Azure, GCP, GitHub, Together, OpenAI

**Core architecture / call chain:**
1. CLI entry (`genai_bench/cli/cli.py`) — `benchmark`, `excel`, `plot` commands
2. CLI options grouped (`option_groups.py`), validation (`validation.py`)
3. Auth: `UnifiedAuthFactory` → model auth + storage auth providers (provider pattern)
4. User classes (Locust HttpUser subclasses): `BaseUser` → `OpenAIUser`, `OCIOpenAIUser`, etc. Each defines `supported_tasks` and `@task` methods
5. Sampling: `Sampler` base → `TextSampler`, `ImageSampler`. `Sampler.create(task)` factory.
6. Scenarios: `Scenario` base with registry, `from_string` factory → `NormalDistribution`, `UniformDistribution`, `DeterministicDistribution`, `EmbeddingScenario`, `ReRankScenario`, `MultimodalScenario`, `DatasetScenario`
7. Data loading: `DataLoaderFactory` → `TextDatasetLoader`, `ImageDatasetLoader`; `DatasetConfig` supports file/huggingface/custom
8. Protocol: Pydantic models for requests, responses, ExperimentMetadata
9. Metrics: `RequestMetricsCollector` (per-request), `AggregatedMetricsCollector` (per-run), `RequestLevelMetrics`/`AggregatedMetrics`/`MetricStats`/`StatField`
10. Distributed: `DistributedRunner` — master/worker/local, message handlers, multiprocessing
11. UI: `Dashboard` (`RichLiveDashboard`/`MinimalDashboard`) + layout + plots
12. Storage: `StorageFactory` → multi-cloud storage providers (`BaseStorage`)
13. Analysis: `experiment_loader` (load JSON), `excel_report` (Excel workbook), `plot_config` (config + presets), `flexible_plot_report` + `plot_report`
14. Time units: `TimeUnitConverter` (s/ms conversion)
15. Logging: Rich-based handlers, worker logging

**Learning path design:**

Unit 1 (Beginner): Getting started — what it is, install, run first benchmark, CLI structure, directory layout, protocol basics
Unit 2 (Beginner→Intermediate): Configuration & data — scenarios, tasks, data loading, sampling
Unit 3 (Intermediate): Core execution — users (backends), metrics collection, distributed runner
Unit 4 (Intermediate): Auth & storage — multi-cloud auth providers, storage providers
Unit 5 (Intermediate): Analysis — experiment loader, excel report, plots
Unit 6 (Advanced): UI dashboard, time units, logging, distributed internals, extension/contribution

Let me be careful to keep each lecture focused on one topic with 2-5 minimal modules, real source files, and a practice task.

Let me design the lectures:

**Unit 1 — 入门: 认识 genai-bench 并跑通第一个基准 (beginner)**
- u1-l1: 项目总览与定位 — README, pyproject, what is genai-bench, the benchmarking concept. source: README.md, pyproject.toml, genai_bench/version.py, genai_bench/__init__.py
- u1-l2: 安装与运行方式 — install, CLI entry, Makefile, Dockerfile. source: pyproject.toml, genai_bench/cli/cli.py, Makefile, Dockerfile
- u1-l3: 目录结构与模块全景 — directory layout, the package modules overview. source: genai_bench/* overview, docs
- u1-l4: CLI 入口与三大命令 (benchmark/excel/plot) — cli.py group, add_command. source: genai_bench/cli/cli.py, genai_bench/cli/report.py
- u1-l5: 协议数据模型 (protocol.py) — UserRequest/Response, ExperimentMetadata, Pydantic. source: genai_bench/protocol.py

**Unit 2 — 配置、场景与数据采样 (beginner→intermediate)**
- u2-l1: Task 任务定义 — input-to-output modality, supported tasks. source: validation.py (supported_tasks maps), docs/getting-started/task-definition.md, sampling/base.py
- u2-l2: Traffic Scenario 场景定义与解析 — Scenario base, registry, from_string, distributions. source: scenarios/base.py, scenarios/text.py, scenarios/multimodal.py
- u2-l3: 数据集加载 (DatasetConfig + loaders) — DatasetConfig, file/hf/custom, DataLoaderFactory, loaders. source: data/config.py, data/loaders/factory.py, data/loaders/text.py, data/loaders/image.py, data/loaders/base.py
- u2-l4: 采样器 Sampler 与请求构造 — Sampler base, TextSampler, sampling flow, prefix caching. source: sampling/base.py, sampling/text.py, sampling/image.py

**Unit 3 — 核心执行链: User 与指标采集 (intermediate)**
- u3-l1: User 基类与 Locust 集成 — BaseUser, HttpUser, supported_tasks, sample, collect_metrics. source: user/base_user.py, user/openai_user.py
- u3-l2: OpenAIUser 请求与响应解析 (流式/非流式) — chat streaming parsing, embeddings, rerank, images, speech. source: user/openai_user.py, protocol.py
- u3-l3: 多后端 User 体系 — OCI, AWS Bedrock, Azure, GCP, Together users, backend mapping. source: user/* , validation.py API_BACKEND_USER_MAP
- u3-l4: 单请求指标计算 RequestMetricsCollector — ttft, tpot, throughput formulas. source: metrics/request_metrics_collector.py, metrics/metrics.py
- u3-l5: 运行级指标聚合 AggregatedMetricsCollector — aggregate, percentiles, warmup/cooldown, save. source: metrics/aggregated_metrics_collector.py, metrics/metrics.py

U4-L2: 运行级聚合 AggregatedMetricsCollector

U4-L3: 指标数据模型与时间单位转换

input.head: 7fd04d8c53b1df20450019fb7eedec0c4caec24f
input.rationale: genai-bench 是一个基于 Locust 的 LLM 服务 token 级基准测试工具，核心由「CLI 编排 + 多后端 User + 场景/采样 + 指标采集聚合 + 分布式运行 + 实时 UI + 多云认证存储 + 实验分析报告」几大子系统构成。学习路线据此分成 8 个单元、共 30 篇讲义，按依赖关系与难度递进：U1 入门层先让读者跑通项目并建立整体地图；U2–U6 进阶层依次深入「任务/场景/数据采样」「User 后端执行」「指标计算与聚合」「多云认证与存储」「实验分析与报告」这条从输入到产出的主链路；U7–U8 专家层再讲分布式主从架构、实时 UI、日志，以及把所有子系统串起来的 benchmark 主流程编排、扩展开发、测试/CI/发布。第一单元从零开始覆盖定位、安装、目录与入口；后续单元各自聚焦一个相对独立子系统，每篇讲义只解决一个主题，引用 1–6 个真实源码文件并配一个可操作的代码实践任务，讲义之间通过 depends_on 明确前置依赖，最终由 U8-L1 的 benchmark 主流程作为贯穿全局的 capstone。full 模式下所有讲义 action 均为 "new"。

units.title: 入门：认识 genai-bench 并跑通第一个基准

lectures.title: 项目总览与定位
lectures.topic: genai-bench 是什么、解决什么问题、核心特性（CLI/Live UI/分析报告）与整体技术栈。
learning_goals: 理解 LLM 服务基准测试的概念与 token 级指标的意义
说出 genai-bench 的三大能力：CLI 工具、实时 UI 仪表盘、实验分析器
识别项目的关键依赖与版本信息
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 README 与 pyproject.toml，用 pip install genai-bench 安装，运行 genai-bench --version 确认版本，并列出 genai-bench 依赖的 5 个关键库及其作用。

lectures.title: 安装与运行第一个基准
lectures.topic: 如何安装 genai-bench，以及如何用 benchmark 子命令对模型发起一次最小基准测试。
learning_goals: 掌握 pip 安装与可选依赖（aws/azure/gcp/multi-cloud）安装方式
能读懂 README 中的快速开始命令并完成一次 text-to-text 基准
理解 benchmark 命令关键参数的含义与运行产出物
lectures.practice_task: 启动一个本地 OpenAI 兼容服务（或用占位地址 dry-run），执行 README 中的 text-to-text benchmark 命令，观察生成的 experiments 目录与 summary 文件结构。

lectures.title: 目录结构与模块全景
lectures.topic: genai-bench 仓库的目录组织、各子包职责与源码地图。
learning_goals: 画出 genai_bench 包的目录结构
说明 cli/user/sampling/scenarios/metrics/auth/storage/analysis/ui/distributed 各包职责
定位每个子系统的入口文件
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 cli.py 顶部的 import，绘制一张从 cli 到各子系统的依赖草图，标注每个 import 对应的功能模块。

lectures.title: CLI 入口与三大命令
lectures.topic: 用 click 定义的 cli 组，以及 benchmark/excel/plot 三个子命令的注册与职责。
learning_goals: 理解 click group 与子命令注册机制
区分 benchmark/excel/plot 三命令各自的作用
看懂 cli.py 中 add_command 的组织方式以及 report 模块的复用
minimal_modules: report 模块（excel/plot 命令实现）
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 report.py，分别说明 excel 和 plot 子命令接收哪些参数，并写一段说明它们如何复用 analysis 子系统。

lectures.title: 协议数据模型（protocol.py）
lectures.topic: 用 Pydantic 定义的请求/响应/实验元数据模型，是各模块间的统一数据契约。
learning_goals: 理解 UserRequest/UserResponse 及其子类的字段含义
掌握 ExperimentMetadata 的关键字段
认识 Pydantic 在项目中作为数据契约的作用
lectures.practice_task: 基于 protocol.py 写一个小脚本，构造一个 UserChatRequest 和一个 ExperimentMetadata 实例并 model_dump_json 打印，验证字段校验行为。

units.title: 进阶：任务、场景与数据采样

lectures.title: Task 任务定义体系
lectures.topic: input-to-output 任务命名约定、各后端支持的任务集合与默认场景映射。
learning_goals: 理解 "<input>-to-<output>" 任务格式
知道 text-to-text/embeddings/rerank/image/speech 等任务
看懂 validate_task 的校验逻辑与后端任务限制
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_BY_TASK，为某个自定义任务设想一个合理场景字符串，并解释为何 image 类任务需要数据集。

lectures.title: Traffic Scenario 场景定义与解析
lectures.topic: 场景字符串（N/D/U/E/R/I/A/dataset）的语法、Scenario 基类的注册表与 from_string 工厂。
learning_goals: 掌握各类场景字符串的含义与正则校验
理解 Scenario 基类与 __init_subclass__ 自动注册机制
能用 from_string 解析任意场景字符串
minimal_modules: 注册表与 from_string 工厂
lectures.practice_task: 写脚本调用 Scenario.from_string 解析 "N(480,240)/(300,150)"、"E(1024)"、"I(1024,1024)"，并打印各自 sample() 的结果。

lectures.title: 数据集加载（DatasetConfig 与 Loaders）
lectures.topic: 支持本地文件 / HuggingFace / 自定义的数据集配置与加载工厂。
learning_goals: 理解 DatasetConfig 与 DatasetSourceConfig 的字段
掌握 DataLoaderFactory 按输入模态分发的逻辑
了解 TextDatasetLoader/ImageDatasetLoader 的数据处理方式
lectures.practice_task: 用 from_cli_args 构造一个指向内置 sonnet.txt 的 DatasetConfig，再用 DataLoaderFactory.load_data_for_task 加载 text-to-text 数据并打印前几条。

lectures.title: 采样器 Sampler 与请求构造
lectures.topic: Sampler 基类与 create 工厂、TextSampler 如何结合场景生成 UserRequest，以及 prefix 缓存机制。
learning_goals: 理解 Sampler 的 modality_registry 与 create(task) 工厂
掌握 TextSampler.sample 按 output_modality 分发的流程
了解 prefix-len / prefix-ratio 的共享前缀缓存
lectures.practice_task: 构造一个 TextSampler，分别用 D(100,100) 和 dataset 模式调用 sample，对比返回的 UserChatRequest 字段差异。

units.title: 进阶：User 后端体系与请求执行

lectures.title: User 基类与 Locust 集成
lectures.topic: BaseUser 作为 Locust HttpUser 子类、supported_tasks、sample 与 collect_metrics 机制。
learning_goals: 理解 genai-bench 如何基于 Locust 构造虚拟用户
掌握 sample() 从 environment.sampler 取请求的流程
理解 collect_metrics 如何把指标上报给 locust events 与 runner
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 base_user.py，画出一次请求从 sample() 到 send_message('request_metrics') 的完整时序，并指出关键扩展点。

lectures.title: OpenAIUser 流式与非流式响应解析
lectures.topic: OpenAIUser 对 chat/embeddings/rerank/images/speech 的请求构造与响应解析，重点解析 SSE 流式 chunk。
learning_goals: 掌握 chat 流式解析中 TTFT、tokens、reasoning_tokens 的提取
理解不同任务的 parse 策略
了解 usage 信息缺失时的 tokenizer 回退估算逻辑
lectures.practice_task: 写一个最小函数，给定一段模拟的 SSE data 行列表，复用解析思路统计 tokens_received 与 time_at_first_token。

lectures.title: 多后端 User 体系
lectures.topic: OpenAI / OCI / AWS Bedrock / Azure / GCP / Together 等多后端 User 类与 API_BACKEND_USER_MAP 映射。
learning_goals: 理解各后端 User 的差异与共同基类
掌握 validate_api_backend 如何选择 user_class
知道 vllm/sglang 复用 OpenAIUser 的原因
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 validation.py 的 API_BACKEND_USER_MAP，选择一个非 OpenAI 后端（如 aws-bedrock），说明它复用了哪些基类方法、覆写了哪些。

lectures.title: 单请求指标计算 RequestMetricsCollector
lectures.topic: 从 UserResponse 计算 TTFT / TPOT / 吞吐等请求级指标的公式。
learning_goals: 掌握 ttft/e2e_latency/output_latency 的计算
理解 tpot 与 output_inference_speed 的关系
区分 chat/tts/image/embeddings 的指标重置逻辑
minimal_modules: 非聊天任务的指标重置
lectures.practice_task: 构造一个 UserChatResponse（给定时间戳与 token 数），调用 RequestMetricsCollector.calculate_metrics，验证 ttft/tpot/throughput 的数值。

lectures.title: 运行级聚合 AggregatedMetricsCollector
lectures.topic: 对一个 [scenario, concurrency] 运行的所有请求做统计聚合、warmup/cooldown 过滤与保存。
learning_goals: 理解 aggregate_metrics_data 的百分位与吞吐聚合
掌握 warmup/cooldown 过滤机制
了解 save 的 JSON 结构与 filter_metrics 异常值处理
lectures.practice_task: 构造若干 RequestLevelMetrics 喂给 collector，调用 aggregate_metrics_data，检查 stats 的 p50/p99 与 mean_output_throughput。

lectures.title: 指标数据模型与时间单位转换
lectures.topic: RequestLevelMetrics/AggregatedMetrics/MetricStats/StatField 的分层结构，以及 s↔ms 单位转换。
learning_goals: 理解分层指标模型的字段与序列化
掌握 TimeUnitConverter 对延迟字段的转换
了解 metrics_time_unit 如何贯穿保存/UI/报告
lectures.practice_task: 用 TimeUnitConverter.convert_metrics_dict 把一份聚合指标从 s 转成 ms，验证 ttft/e2e_latency 放大 1000 倍而 token 数不变。

lectures.title: 认证体系总览与 UnifiedAuthFactory
lectures.topic: 模型认证与存储认证两套 provider 抽象，以及统一工厂 UnifiedAuthFactory。

理解 UnifiedAuthFactory 的两个 create 方法
认识 adapter 模式（OCI/OpenAI/Together）
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 unified_factory.py，列出 create_model_auth 支持的 provider 及其各自构造的类，画出 provider→adapter 关系图。

lectures.title: 模型认证 Provider 实现
lectures.topic: OpenAI / OCI / Bedrock / Azure / GCP / Together 等模型认证 provider 的具体实现。
learning_goals: 理解 get_headers/get_config/get_auth_type 的实现差异

lectures.practice_task: 选择一个 provider（如 AzureOpenAIAuth），实现/补全一个返回 headers 的 mock，验证 get_config 输出符合预期。

lectures.title: 存储认证与多云存储
lectures.topic: StorageFactory、BaseStorage 抽象与各云存储 provider 的上传/下载能力。
learning_goals: 理解 BaseStorage 的统一接口（upload/download/list/delete）
掌握 StorageFactory 的 lazy import 与 provider-auth 校验
了解 upload_folder 在结果上传中的作用

lectures.title: Excel 报告生成

learning_goals: 理解 create_workbook 的输入与 percentile 选择

lectures.title: 绘图配置系统 plot_config

learning_goals: 理解 plot 配置模型与校验（单/多 y_field、position）

了解 get_field_value 按 stats.ttft.mean 路径取值
lectures.practice_task: 编写一个自定义 2x2 plot 配置 JSON，用 PlotConfigManager.load_from_file 加载并打印每个 PlotSpec。

lectures.title: 灵活绘图与 plot 命令
lectures.topic: FlexiblePlotGenerator 按 group_key 生成图、plot_report 的单场景图与 plot 子命令串联。
learning_goals: 理解 group_key（traffic_scenario/none/自定义）的分组绘图
掌握 plot_experiment_data_flexible 的调用
了解 plot 命令如何组合 config + loader
lectures.practice_task: 用 examples/plot_configs 中的配置，对一份实验数据运行 plot 命令（或脚本），生成 PNG 并对照 PlotSpec 解释每幅图。

units.title: 专家：分布式运行、实时 UI 与日志

lectures.title: DistributedRunner 主从架构
lectures.topic: 基于 Locust master/worker 与 multiprocessing 的分布式压测架构、消息处理与清理。
learning_goals: 理解 master/worker/local 三种模式与 setup 流程
掌握 update_scenario/request_metrics 等消息的收发
了解 CPU 亲和、worker 日志与资源清理
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 _register_message_handlers，画出 master 与 worker 各自注册哪些 handler、互相发送哪些消息的流程图。

lectures.title: 实时仪表盘 Dashboard
lectures.topic: 基于 rich 的实时仪表盘，展示指标面板、直方图、散点图与进度条。

lectures.title: 日志系统
lectures.topic: 基于 rich 的日志 handler、缓冲 flush，以及 worker 日志跨进程传递。
learning_goals: 理解 RollingRichPanelHandler / DelayedRichHandler 的作用
掌握 WorkerLoggingManager 把 worker 日志经队列回传 master
了解日志同时落终端与文件的机制
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 logging.py，解释为何在 Live 上下文中要延迟 flush 日志，并说明 worker 日志如何不污染各自终端。

units.title: 专家：主流程编排、扩展与工程实践

lectures.title: benchmark 主流程编排（capstone）
lectures.topic: cli.py benchmark 命令如何串联认证→采样→环境→分布式→双层循环→报告→上传的完整流程。
learning_goals: 理解 benchmark 函数的整体阶段划分
掌握 scenario×iteration 双层循环与每次 run 的指标收集

lectures.practice_task: 在 cli.py 中为 benchmark 的每个阶段加上注释标签（认证/数据/采样/运行/报告/上传），并用一张时序图描述一次完整实验的数据流。

lectures.title: CLI 选项分组与校验机制
lectures.topic: option_groups 的选项分组、validation 中的回调式校验与跨参数约束。
learning_goals: 理解 api_options/sampling_options 等分组如何组合到 benchmark
掌握 validate_tokenizer / validate_prefix_options 等校验
了解 iteration_type 与 task 的联动
lectures.practice_task: 阅读前缀校验逻辑，构造一组会触发 --prefix-len / --prefix-ratio 报错的参数组合，解释每条错误的触发条件。

lectures.title: 扩展指南：添加新后端 / 任务 / 场景
lectures.topic: 如何在不破坏既有架构的前提下新增一个模型后端、任务或场景。
learning_goals: 理解新增 User 子类并注册到 API_BACKEND_USER_MAP 的步骤
掌握新增 Sampler / Scenario 通过注册表自动生效的机制
了解官方扩展文档的约定
lectures.practice_task: 仿照 OpenAIUser 实现一个极简的 CustomUser 子类（仅 text-to-text），并说明需要改哪些注册点才能让它被 --api-backend 选中。

lectures.title: 测试体系、CI 与发布
lectures.topic: pytest 测试组织、CI 流水线、代码风格（ruff/mypy/pre-commit）与 Docker / 发布流程。
learning_goals: 了解 tests 目录与 conftest 的组织
理解 CI（ci.yml）与 release.yml 的流程
掌握 Docker 运行方式与版本管理
lectures.practice_task: 运行 make（或 pytest）执行测试套件，阅读 ci.yml 说明它跑了哪些检查，并尝试用 Dockerfile 构建镜像运行一次基准。

data.head: 7fd04d8c53b1df20450019fb7eedec0c4caec24f
data.rationale: genai-bench 是一个基于 Locust 的 LLM 服务 token 级基准测试工具，核心由「CLI 编排 + 多后端 User + 场景/采样 + 指标采集聚合 + 分布式运行 + 实时 UI + 多云认证存储 + 实验分析报告」几大子系统构成。学习路线据此分成 8 个单元、共 30 篇讲义，按依赖关系与难度递进：U1 入门层先让读者跑通项目并建立整体地图；U2–U6 进阶层依次深入「任务/场景/数据采样」「User 后端执行」「指标计算与聚合」「多云认证与存储」「实验分析与报告」这条从输入到产出的主链路；U7–U8 专家层再讲分布式主从架构、实时 UI、日志，以及把所有子系统串起来的 benchmark 主流程编排、扩展开发、测试/CI/发布。第一单元从零开始覆盖定位、安装、目录与入口；后续单元各自聚焦一个相对独立子系统，每篇讲义只解决一个主题，引用 1–6 个真实源码文件并配一个可操作的代码实践任务，讲义之间通过 depends_on 明确前置依赖，最终由 U8-L1 的 benchmark 主流程作为贯穿全局的 capstone。full 模式下所有讲义 action 均为 "new"。

units.title: 入门：认识 genai-bench 并跑通第一个基准

lectures.title: 项目总览与定位
lectures.topic: genai-bench 是什么、解决什么问题、核心特性（CLI/Live UI/分析报告）与整体技术栈。
learning_goals: 理解 LLM 服务基准测试的概念与 token 级指标的意义
说出 genai-bench 的三大能力：CLI 工具、实时 UI 仪表盘、实验分析器
识别项目的关键依赖与版本信息
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 README 与 pyproject.toml，用 pip install genai-bench 安装，运行 genai-bench --version 确认版本，并列出 genai-bench 依赖的 5 个关键库及其作用。

lectures.title: 安装与运行第一个基准
lectures.topic: 如何安装 genai-bench，以及如何用 benchmark 子命令对模型发起一次最小基准测试。
learning_goals: 掌握 pip 安装与可选依赖（aws/azure/gcp/multi-cloud）安装方式
能读懂 README 中的快速开始命令并完成一次 text-to-text 基准
理解 benchmark 命令关键参数的含义与运行产出物
lectures.practice_task: 启动一个本地 OpenAI 兼容服务（或用占位地址 dry-run），执行 README 中的 text-to-text benchmark 命令，观察生成的 experiments 目录与 summary 文件结构。

lectures.title: 目录结构与模块全景
lectures.topic: genai-bench 仓库的目录组织、各子包职责与源码地图。
learning_goals: 画出 genai_bench 包的目录结构
说明 cli/user/sampling/scenarios/metrics/auth/storage/analysis/ui/distributed 各包职责
定位每个子系统的入口文件
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 cli.py 顶部的 import，绘制一张从 cli 到各子系统的依赖草图，标注每个 import 对应的功能模块。

lectures.title: CLI 入口与三大命令
lectures.topic: 用 click 定义的 cli 组，以及 benchmark/excel/plot 三个子命令的注册与职责。
learning_goals: 理解 click group 与子命令注册机制
区分 benchmark/excel/plot 三命令各自的作用
看懂 cli.py 中 add_command 的组织方式以及 report 模块的复用
minimal_modules: report 模块（excel/plot 命令实现）
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 report.py，分别说明 excel 和 plot 子命令接收哪些参数，并写一段说明它们如何复用 analysis 子系统。

lectures.title: 协议数据模型（protocol.py）
lectures.topic: 用 Pydantic 定义的请求/响应/实验元数据模型，是各模块间的统一数据契约。
learning_goals: 理解 UserRequest/UserResponse 及其子类的字段含义
掌握 ExperimentMetadata 的关键字段
认识 Pydantic 在项目中作为数据契约的作用
lectures.practice_task: 基于 protocol.py 写一个小脚本，构造一个 UserChatRequest 和一个 ExperimentMetadata 实例并 model_dump_json 打印，验证字段校验行为。

units.title: 进阶：任务、场景与数据采样

lectures.title: Task 任务定义体系
lectures.topic: input-to-output 任务命名约定、各后端支持的任务集合与默认场景映射。
learning_goals: 理解 "<input>-to-<output>" 任务格式
知道 text-to-text/embeddings/rerank/image/speech 等任务
看懂 validate_task 的校验逻辑与后端任务限制
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 DEFAULT_SCENARIOS_BY_TASK，为某个自定义任务设想一个合理场景字符串，并解释为何 image 类任务需要数据集。

lectures.title: Traffic Scenario 场景定义与解析
lectures.topic: 场景字符串（N/D/U/E/R/I/A/dataset）的语法、Scenario 基类的注册表与 from_string 工厂。
learning_goals: 掌握各类场景字符串的含义与正则校验

能用 from_string 解析任意场景字符串
minimal_modules: 注册表与 from_string 工厂
lectures.practice_task: 写脚本调用 Scenario.from_string 解析 "N(480,240)/(300,150)"、"E(1024)"、"I(1024,1024)"，并打印各自 sample() 的结果。

lectures.title: 数据集加载（DatasetConfig 与 Loaders）

learning_goals: 理解 DatasetConfig 与 DatasetSourceConfig 的字段
掌握 DataLoaderFactory 按输入模态分发的逻辑
了解 TextDatasetLoader/ImageDatasetLoader 的数据处理方式
lectures.practice_task: 用 from_cli_args 构造一个指向内置 sonnet.txt 的 DatasetConfig，再用 DataLoaderFactory.load_data_for_task 加载 text-to-text 数据并打印前几条。

lectures.title: 采样器 Sampler 与请求构造
lectures.topic: Sampler 基类与 create 工厂、TextSampler 如何结合场景生成 UserRequest，以及 prefix 缓存机制。
learning_goals: 理解 Sampler 的 modality_registry 与 create(task) 工厂
掌握 TextSampler.sample 按 output_modality 分发的流程

lectures.practice_task: 构造一个 TextSampler，分别用 D(100,100) 和 dataset 模式调用 sample，对比返回的 UserChatRequest 字段差异。

units.title: 进阶：User 后端体系与请求执行

了解 usage 信息缺失时的 tokenizer 回退估算逻辑

lectures.topic: OpenAI / OCI / AWS Bedrock / Azure / GCP / Together 等多后端 User 类与 API_BACKEND_USER_MAP 映射。
learning_goals: 理解各后端 User 的差异与共同基类
掌握 validate_api_backend 如何选择 user_class

lectures.practice_task: 阅读 validation.py 的 API_BACKEND_USER_MAP，选择一个非 OpenAI 后端（如 aws-bedrock），说明它复用了哪些基类方法、覆写了哪些。

lectures.title: 单请求指标计算 RequestMetricsCollector
lectures.topic: 从 UserResponse 计算 TTFT / TPOT / 吞吐等请求级指标的公式。
learning_goals: 掌握 ttft/e2e_latency/output_latency 的计算
理解 tpot 与 output_inference_speed 的关系
区分 chat/tts/image/embeddings 的指标重置逻辑

lectures.topic: 对一个 [scenario, concurrency] 运行的所有请求做统计聚合、warmup/cooldown 过滤与保存。
learning_goals: 理解 aggregate_metrics_data 的百分位与吞吐聚合
掌握 warmup/cooldown 过滤机制

lectures.topic: RequestLevelMetrics/AggregatedMetrics/MetricStats/StatField 的分层结构，以及 s↔ms 单位转换。

lectures.title: 认证体系总览与 UnifiedAuthFactory
lectures.topic: 模型认证与存储认证两套 provider 抽象，以及统一工厂 UnifiedAuthFactory。

lectures.practice_task: 阅读 unified_factory.py，列出 create_model_auth 支持的 provider 及其各自构造的类，画出 provider→adapter 关系图。

lectures.topic: OpenAI / OCI / Bedrock / Azure / GCP / Together 等模型认证 provider 的具体实现。

掌握 OCI/OpenAI 适配器如何包装底层 auth
了解 Azure AD、GCP credentials 等凭据来源
lectures.practice_task: 选择一个 provider（如 AzureOpenAIAuth），实现/补全一个返回 headers 的 mock，验证 get_config 输出符合预期。

lectures.title: 存储认证与多云存储
lectures.topic: StorageFactory、BaseStorage 抽象与各云存储 provider 的上传/下载能力。
learning_goals: 理解 BaseStorage 的统一接口（upload/download/list/delete）
掌握 StorageFactory 的 lazy import 与 provider-auth 校验
了解 upload_folder 在结果上传中的作用
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 cli.py 末尾 upload 逻辑，说明 --storage-provider/--storage-bucket 如何驱动 StorageFactory 并上传实验文件夹。

lectures.title: OCI 认证与对象存储深入
lectures.topic: OCI 多种认证方式（user/instance principal、obo token）与对象存储 datastore 细节。
learning_goals: 理解 OCI session 与多种 auth type
掌握 OCIStorageAuthAdapter / OCIModelAuthAdapter 的封装
了解 oci_object_storage 的 datastore 与 object_uri
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 session.py，列出 OCI 支持的认证方式，并解释 instance principal 与 user principal 的适用场景。

lectures.title: 实验结果加载 experiment_loader
lectures.topic: 从实验目录读取 experiment_metadata.json 与各 run JSON，组织为 scenario×concurrency 结构。
learning_goals: 理解 load_one_experiment/load_multiple_experiments 的输出结构
掌握 filter_criteria 过滤
了解缺失场景/并发级别的告警逻辑
lectures.practice_task: 用 tests/analysis 下的 mock 数据或自有实验，调用 load_one_experiment 打印 run_data 的 scenario→concurrency 结构。

lectures.title: Excel 报告生成
lectures.topic: 用 openpyxl 把聚合指标写成多 sheet 的 Excel 报告。
learning_goals: 理解 create_workbook 的输入与 percentile 选择
了解 sheet 结构、空列折叠与表头重命名
知道 SCENARIO_MAP 与定价信息的作用
lectures.practice_task: 运行 examples/experiment_excel.py（或用 mock 数据）生成一个 Excel，打开并说明各 sheet 的含义。

lectures.title: 绘图配置系统 plot_config
lectures.topic: PlotSpec/PlotConfig/PlotLayout 的声明式绘图配置、内置 preset 与字段路径解析。
learning_goals: 理解 plot 配置模型与校验（单/多 y_field、position）
掌握 preset（2x4_default 等）的用法
了解 get_field_value 按 stats.ttft.mean 路径取值
lectures.practice_task: 编写一个自定义 2x2 plot 配置 JSON，用 PlotConfigManager.load_from_file 加载并打印每个 PlotSpec。

lectures.title: 灵活绘图与 plot 命令
lectures.topic: FlexiblePlotGenerator 按 group_key 生成图、plot_report 的单场景图与 plot 子命令串联。
learning_goals: 理解 group_key（traffic_scenario/none/自定义）的分组绘图
掌握 plot_experiment_data_flexible 的调用
了解 plot 命令如何组合 config + loader
lectures.practice_task: 用 examples/plot_configs 中的配置，对一份实验数据运行 plot 命令（或脚本），生成 PNG 并对照 PlotSpec 解释每幅图。

units.title: 专家：分布式运行、实时 UI 与日志

lectures.title: DistributedRunner 主从架构
lectures.topic: 基于 Locust master/worker 与 multiprocessing 的分布式压测架构、消息处理与清理。
learning_goals: 理解 master/worker/local 三种模式与 setup 流程
掌握 update_scenario/request_metrics 等消息的收发
了解 CPU 亲和、worker 日志与资源清理
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 _register_message_handlers，画出 master 与 worker 各自注册哪些 handler、互相发送哪些消息的流程图。

lectures.title: 实时仪表盘 Dashboard
lectures.topic: 基于 rich 的实时仪表盘，展示指标面板、直方图、散点图与进度条。
learning_goals: 理解 RichLiveDashboard 与 MinimalDashboard 的切换（ENABLE_UI）
掌握 handle_single_request 的进度与面板更新
了解 layout/plots 子模块的组成
lectures.practice_task: 设置 ENABLE_UI=false 运行一次基准，对比 MinimalDashboard 与 RichLiveDashboard 的行为差异，说明 no-op 设计的好处。

lectures.title: 日志系统
lectures.topic: 基于 rich 的日志 handler、缓冲 flush，以及 worker 日志跨进程传递。
learning_goals: 理解 RollingRichPanelHandler / DelayedRichHandler 的作用
掌握 WorkerLoggingManager 把 worker 日志经队列回传 master
了解日志同时落终端与文件的机制
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 logging.py，解释为何在 Live 上下文中要延迟 flush 日志，并说明 worker 日志如何不污染各自终端。

units.title: 专家：主流程编排、扩展与工程实践

lectures.title: benchmark 主流程编排（capstone）
lectures.topic: cli.py benchmark 命令如何串联认证→采样→环境→分布式→双层循环→报告→上传的完整流程。
learning_goals: 理解 benchmark 函数的整体阶段划分
掌握 scenario×iteration 双层循环与每次 run 的指标收集
看懂结尾自动生成 excel/plot 与可选上传
lectures.practice_task: 在 cli.py 中为 benchmark 的每个阶段加上注释标签（认证/数据/采样/运行/报告/上传），并用一张时序图描述一次完整实验的数据流。

lectures.title: CLI 选项分组与校验机制
lectures.topic: option_groups 的选项分组、validation 中的回调式校验与跨参数约束。
learning_goals: 理解 api_options/sampling_options 等分组如何组合到 benchmark
掌握 validate_tokenizer / validate_prefix_options 等校验
了解 iteration_type 与 task 的联动
lectures.practice_task: 阅读前缀校验逻辑，构造一组会触发 --prefix-len / --prefix-ratio 报错的参数组合，解释每条错误的触发条件。

lectures.title: 扩展指南：添加新后端 / 任务 / 场景
lectures.topic: 如何在不破坏既有架构的前提下新增一个模型后端、任务或场景。
learning_goals: 理解新增 User 子类并注册到 API_BACKEND_USER_MAP 的步骤
掌握新增 Sampler / Scenario 通过注册表自动生效的机制
了解官方扩展文档的约定
lectures.practice_task: 仿照 OpenAIUser 实现一个极简的 CustomUser 子类（仅 text-to-text），并说明需要改哪些注册点才能让它被 --api-backend 选中。

lectures.title: 测试体系、CI 与发布
lectures.topic: pytest 测试组织、CI 流水线、代码风格（ruff/mypy/pre-commit）与 Docker / 发布流程。
learning_goals: 了解 tests 目录与 conftest 的组织
理解 CI（ci.yml）与 release.yml 的流程
掌握 Docker 运行方式与版本管理
lectures.practice_task: 运行 make（或 pytest）执行测试套件，阅读 ci.yml 说明它跑了哪些检查，并尝试用 Dockerfile 构建镜像运行一次基准。
